seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel is a 43-year-old male
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel presents for evaluation of back pain
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel has a past medical history of diabetes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel has a past medical history of high blood pressure
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel has a past medical history of high cholesterol
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel developed low back pain after bending over to pick something up while working in the yard
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel's low back pain radiates down his left leg
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel's back and leg pain has been present for about four days
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel reports that his back pain has not been improving over the past four days
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel reports tingling in certain positions associated with his leg symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel denies weakness in his legs
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel reports that pain is his predominant symptom in the back and leg
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel denies loss of bowel function
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel denies loss of bladder function
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel has had occasional back pain over the years without prior back surgery
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel has taken ibuprofen for his current back pain with some relief
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel is taking metformin 500 mg twice daily for diabetes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel checks his blood sugar two or three times per week
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel's home blood sugars are usually in the 120 to 140 mg/dL range
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel reports taking his diabetes medicine as prescribed
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel reports his diet is only "alright" and that he is about 15 pounds overweight
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel believes his blood sugar control has been okay
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel's last hemoglobin A1c was 7.1%
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician assesses Gabriel's diabetes control as good but could be better
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel is trying to reduce carbohydrate and sweet intake but still eats cookies his wife makes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician counsels Gabriel that diet and exercise are important to prevent diabetes complications such as kidney failure and eye problems
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to order a repeat hemoglobin A1c for Gabriel today
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel's clinic blood pressure today is approximately 120/70 mmHg
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel is taking Norvasc 5 mg once daily for hypertension
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel checks his blood pressure about once a week or two to three times per month
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel's home blood pressures are usually 120-130/70-80 mmHg
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel reports no major problems with his blood pressure control
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel reports swelling in his ankles
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel's ankle swelling began about one to two months after starting Norvasc
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Gabriel wonders if his ankle swelling is related to Norvasc
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician states that Norvasc can cause ankle swelling and that it could be related in Gabriel's case
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Neck exam shows no jugular venous distention
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Neck exam reveals no bruits
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Lung exam reveals no rales or wheezing
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Heart exam shows a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, previously known, with otherwise regular rate and rhythm
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Abdominal exam is soft
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Back exam shows tenderness in the left paraspinal lower back area
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Straight leg raise test is negative
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Neurological exam is normal
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Extremity exam shows 1+ nonpitting edema of the lower extremities with ankle swelling
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician
